feeItems.note: Representative current fee per professional paper.

feeItems.label: Advanced exam attempt
feeItems.note: Current advanced/case-study range; the complete new-route total was not published as one price.

qualifications.costSummary: No honest single current total: annual student fees, 14 exam attempts, VAT, learning and possible resits vary, and the authorised employer commonly funds some or all costs. Published component fees are exposed instead.
qualifications.exemptions: Credits may cover up to twelve exams based on eligible prior learning, but not the advanced case-study requirements; exact mapping and fees must be checked.
qualifications.membershipAndMaintenance: Admission follows exams, 450 days, specialised learning, skills and ethics sign-off. An ICAEW practising certificate is a separate member rule for public practice and does not make all accountancy work reserved by law.
barrierNotes: The authorised employer controls access to the training agreement, work opportunities, records and sign-off.
Employer dependency can be more restrictive than the exams themselves.
The Next Generation route applies to new starters after July 2025; earlier students may be on a different version.
qualifications.leastFrictionRoute: Secure an authorised-employer apprenticeship or training contract that pays the fees, then keep the training file and 450-day record current; prior qualifications can reduce exams but cannot remove employer sign-off.
notEquivalentTo: A universal legal requirement to prepare accounts or give tax advice.
Automatic statutory-audit, insolvency or legal authorisation.

qualifications.name: ACCA Qualification
qualifications.level: Chartered certified accountancy qualification under ACCA rules
entryRequirements: Standard school/degree routes are available.
Applicants without the direct-entry academic profile can start through Foundations, which has no minimum academic entry requirement.
Register and pay the annual student subscription.

assessments.name: Strategic Professional option one
assessments.choice: Choose two from AFM, APM, ATX and AAA.

qualifications.experienceRequirement: Thirty-six months' relevant experience and nine performance objectives. Time can span employers, but competence normally needs sign-off by a qualified accountant or IFAC member; unsupervised self-employed public practice does not count.
qualifications.typicalDuration: Typically three to four years while working, but flexible sequencing and exemptions change the duration.

feeItems.label: Initial registration
feeItems.note: 2026 UK registration.

feeItems.label: Annual student subscription
feeItems.note: Payable while registered.

feeItems.label: Ethics and Professional Skills Module
feeItems.note: Current module fee.

feeItems.label: Applied Skills exam
feeItems.note: Representative September 2026 standard-entry fee; on-demand centre fees vary.

feeItems.label: Strategic Business Leader
feeItems.note: Standard entry.

feeItems.label: Strategic Reporting or option exam
feeItems.note: Standard entry per paper.

qualifications.costSummary: No exact national total because Applied Knowledge/on-demand centres set some fees, annual subscriptions depend on duration, exemptions are charged and resits/late booking multiply costs. The component schedule is more truthful than a single first-pass price.
qualifications.exemptions: Up to nine Applied Knowledge and Applied Skills papers may be exempted; no Strategic Professional exemptions. ACCA charges exemption fees, so prior learning saves time but not all body cost.
qualifications.membershipAndMaintenance: Exams, ethics, 36 months and performance-objective sign-off are required for membership. Members entering UK public practice need the relevant ACCA practising certificate, PII, CPD and recurring fees under member rules.
barrierNotes: March 2026 Taxation and Advanced Taxation pass rates were about half, so resit economics matter.
Qualified-supervisor sign-off remains a workplace dependency even though an authorised employer is not compulsory.
The qualification redesign begins in September 2027 and must be represented as a future version.
qualifications.leastFrictionRoute: Start through Foundations if necessary, use the free Study Hub, accumulate supervised experience across any suitable employers while sitting papers, and avoid late-entry fees. Do not start unsupervised self-employment expecting it to satisfy PER.
notEquivalentTo: A statutory monopoly over general accountancy or tax advice.
Automatic public-practice, audit, insolvency or legal permission.

evidence.locator: 2026 UK fee schedule and late fees

qualifications.name: AAT Level 4 professional accounting diploma
qualifications.designation: Qualification can support MAAT admission; it does not itself confer MAAT or an AAT practice licence
entryRequirements: Open entry with Skillcheck or prior-learning routes to select an appropriate starting level.
Learners normally register through or study with an AAT-approved training provider.
Current Level 4 registration closes 31 August 2026; the replacement begins 1 September 2026.

assessments.name: Two current elective units
assessments.choice: Business Tax and Personal Tax are among current options.

qualifications.experienceRequirement: The diploma is assessment-based. MAAT admission separately requires competence evidence; applicants without recent technical experience can use the current eight-module competence pathway.
qualifications.typicalDuration: About twelve to eighteen months for Level 4; earlier levels commonly take six to twelve months each.

feeItems.label: Current Level 4 body fee
feeItems.note: For the route closing to new registrations on 31 August 2026.

feeItems.label: Advertised Level 4 tuition
feeItems.note: AAT's published indicative provider range, not a fixed tariff.

feeItems.label: Replacement Level 4 body fee
feeItems.note: Published future fee for the new qualification.

qualifications.costSummary: Current Level 4 body fee £597 plus AAT's indicative £1,000–£3,000 tuition range. Starting from Level 2 or 3 adds their body/tuition costs; the September 2026 route must not be mixed with the closing version.
qualifications.exemptions: Relevant prior learning generally must be recent, cannot cover more than half a qualification and still attracts assessment-equivalent fees.
qualifications.membershipAndMaintenance: Level 4 can lead to MAAT after competence and suitability checks. A self-employed AAT member needs a separate AAT licence, annual fee, service competence, PII, AML, continuity and CPD under AAT rules.
barrierNotes: Approved-provider dependency adds a commercial layer to an otherwise accessible route.
Exemption fees mean prior learning does not remove equivalent body cost.
The public directory focuses on licensed members, not every AAT member or UK accountant.
qualifications.leastFrictionRoute: Use Skillcheck to avoid repeating lower levels, enter at the highest honest level, study while employed, and choose the cheaper bookkeeping licence only if its limited service categories match the intended practice.
notEquivalentTo: A legal requirement for non-AAT bookkeepers or tax advisers.
An AAT practice licence, MAAT membership or statutory-audit permission.

evidence.locator: Current Level 4 structure, transition and price

qualifications.name: Solicitor admission through the SQE route
qualifications.level: Professional admission route for England and Wales
qualifications.designation: Solicitor after SRA admission; practice additionally requires current status
entryRequirements: Degree or equivalent in any subject.
Satisfy SRA character and suitability rules.
Register for assessments; no preparation course is legally prescribed.

assessments.name: SQE2
assessments.format: Sixteen written and oral stations testing advocacy, interviewing, analysis, drafting, research and writing

qualifications.experienceRequirement: Two years' qualifying work experience in up to four organisations, providing legal services and developing competences, confirmed by an England and Wales solicitor or COLP.
qualifications.typicalDuration: At least two years once QWE is included; an apprenticeship commonly takes five to six years.

feeItems.label: SQE1 current assessment fee
feeItems.note: Current cycle fee.

feeItems.label: SQE2 current assessment fee
feeItems.note: Current cycle fee.

feeItems.label: SQE1 from autumn 2026
feeItems.note: Published future fee.

feeItems.label: SQE2 from autumn 2026
feeItems.note: Published future fee.

qualifications.costSummary: £4,908 for one current attempt at SQE1 and SQE2, rising to £5,092 for the published autumn 2026 fees, before degree/equivalence, preparation, travel, screening, admission and resits.
qualifications.exemptions: Only qualified lawyers can seek SQE exemptions; SQE1 exemptions are rare and cannot be claimed after failing that assessment.
qualifications.membershipAndMaintenance: After SQE, QWE and suitability, apply for admission. Practising then requires an annual certificate, competence maintenance and regulated-firm/insurance arrangements where applicable.
barrierNotes: Kaplan is the sole contracted assessment provider; course providers form a separate market whose supplied claims the SRA does not verify.
Recent SQE1 overall pass rates were 56% and 41%; resit rates were 25% and 20%, so first-attempt price is not typical for every candidate.
Flexible QWE removed the single training-contract form but retained work-access and confirmer dependence.
qualifications.leastFrictionRoute: Accumulate qualifying work while self-studying or using employer-funded preparation, record it across no more than four organisations, secure a confirmer early, and take SQE1 before SQE2. Do not qualify as a solicitor merely to offer unreserved tax advice.
notEquivalentTo: A specialist tax qualification.
Automatic firm authorisation or permission in Scotland/Northern Ireland.

evidence.locator: Assessment delivery and recent pass/resit rates

qualifications.name: Joint Insolvency Examination Board examinations
qualifications.level: Specialist examinations used within statutory insolvency-practitioner authorisation
entryRequirements: Work in insolvency and select an authorising body's route.
Meet that body's education, experience and application requirements; details differ by RPB.
qualifications.experienceRequirement: Substantial higher-level insolvency experience under the chosen RPB's rules; one reviewed ICAEW route combines JIEB with qualifying experience, fitness, PII, bond and monitoring.
qualifications.typicalDuration: Several years of insolvency work plus examinations; there is no single national minimum timeline across all authorisers.
qualifications.costSummary: No single national price. Examination, course, membership, licence, monitoring, AML levy, PII and bond costs depend on the authorising body and provider; quote one body's figures only as an example.
qualifications.exemptions: Authoriser-specific; do not infer an exemption from one RPB applies to another.
qualifications.membershipAndMaintenance: JIEB passes are not the licence. An individual must obtain and renew RPB authorisation, remain fit and proper, maintain PII/bond/CPD and accept monitoring.
barrierNotes: The exam is one layer inside a longer supervised-experience and annual-authorisation stack.
Costs are fragmented among exam, body, insurance, bond and levy providers.
Authorisation is personal; a software company cannot hold the statutory office instead.
qualifications.leastFrictionRoute: Join an insolvency practice first, build the chosen RPB's qualifying case experience while the employer funds JIEB study where possible, and apply only when the fitness, PII, bond and appointment infrastructure are real.
notEquivalentTo: An insolvency-practitioner authorisation by itself.
Permission for a company or unlicensed assistant to accept a statutory office.

gates.name: Choose and establish the trading person
gates.trigger: Trading independently, whether as a sole trader, partnership, LLP or company.
steps: Choose sole trader, partnership, LLP or company based on ownership and liability.
Register the person with HMRC and/or Companies House as that form requires.
Open records and meet recurring tax and filing duties.
requirements: A real proprietor/director/member, service and contact addresses, tax identity and records.
A company currently needs at least one director and shareholder, PSC information, articles and identity-verification information.

feeItems.label: Sole-trader formation
feeItems.note: No formation fee; tax registration/duties still apply.

feeItems.label: Online/software company incorporation
feeItems.note: Current digital fee after the 2 July 2026 change.

feeItems.label: Digital confirmation statement
feeItems.note: At least once each review period for a company/LLP.

gates.timing: Before incorporation-dependent trading; sole traders register on the tax timetable that applies to them.
gates.renewal: The legal person continues, but tax returns, accounts and confirmation statements recur by form.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Non-compliant incorporation can be rejected. Late accounts, returns and tax can produce penalties, prosecution, strike-off or personal liability depending on the form and conduct.
notEquivalentTo: A tax-adviser licence, professional qualification, AML supervision or proof of competence.
gates.leastFriction: A sole trader has the lowest formation cost; a private company is usually the cleaner default for a tax API because it separates the legal person and supports contracting, while not eliminating director responsibility or insurance needs.

gates.name: Employer recruitment gate
gates.trigger: Seeking an employed or funded apprenticeship/training role.
steps: Find a vacancy whose work and qualification match the target.
Meet the employer's application, testing, interview and right-to-work conditions.
Read the training repayment, study-leave and sign-off terms before accepting.
requirements: Employer-defined qualifications, grades, experience and behaviour; these may be stricter than law or the awarding body's own entry rules.
gates.timing: Before employment; selection cycles and available seats are finite.
gates.renewal: Employment and study support continue only under the contract and performance rules.
gates.refusalOrBreach: There is generally no right to a private-sector role; discrimination and employment law still constrain selection and treatment.
notEquivalentTo: A statutory fitness test or universal profession rule.
gates.leastFriction: Use paid apprenticeships and technician roles where the employer funds study and the same work counts as experience. Compare the employer's filters with the body's direct entry rules rather than assuming they are the law.

evidence.locator: Live employer requirements, six places and training terms
Common work and apprenticeship entry routes

gates.name: ICAEW authorised training employer and agreement
gates.trigger: Starting or completing the Next Generation ACA route.
steps: Secure a role with an ICAEW authorised training employer.
Enter the formal training agreement.
Record exams, 450 practical days, specialised learning, skills and ethics reviews.
Obtain employer sign-off for completion.
requirements: An authorised employer, qualifying work opportunities, an assigned reviewer and an accurate training file.
gates.timing: The agreement is normally at least three years and must cover the required practical work.
gates.renewal: Employer authorisation and the individual training agreement must remain valid until sign-off.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Without an authorised agreement and sign-off, exam passes alone do not complete ACA. Transfer arrangements may be needed when employment changes.
notEquivalentTo: A law requiring an authorised employer for ordinary accountancy work.
gates.leastFriction: Choose an employer already authorised and funding the route; verify before joining who owns the training file, what happens on transfer and whether the actual work can deliver 450 days.

gates.name: HMRC Tax Specialist Programme selection
gates.trigger: Seeking appointment to HMRC's Tax Specialist Programme.
steps: Meet the campaign's degree or internal-entry condition.
Complete online tests and application stages.
Pass assessment, pre-employment and security checks.
Complete the structured programme and assessments.
requirements: For the reviewed 2026 external route, at least a 2:2 degree; no prior tax knowledge is required. Internal entry rules differ.
gates.timing: Annual campaign; programme roughly three years three months to four years.
gates.renewal: Employment continues under Civil Service performance, conduct and appointment rules.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Failure at a selection or programme stage can end that application or progression, subject to campaign and employment procedures.
notEquivalentTo: An external tax qualification or permission to exercise HMRC powers after leaving employment.
gates.leastFriction: Apply directly if already holding a 2:2; internal HMRC employees can use the published internal route. The employer pays salary while teaching tax, but competition and public-service checks are the price of entry.

gates.name: Anti-money-laundering supervision for tax/accountancy services
gates.trigger: Providing in-scope tax/accountancy services by way of business, including automated client-specific calculations, unless a precise exemption applies.
steps: Determine the actual service scope and correct supervisor.
Complete a firm-wide risk assessment, policies, controls and procedures.
Identify beneficial owners, officers and managers and premises.
Apply, pass criminality/approval checks and pay before or within the permitted launch window.
Perform customer due diligence, monitoring, reporting, training and annual renewal.
requirements: A correct supervisor, fit/approved responsible people, AML controls, records, training and due diligence.
The all-customers-supervised B2B exception requires every condition, written contracts and operational inclusion in customers' AML systems; membership alone is not enough.

feeItems.label: HMRC application
feeItems.note: Where HMRC is the supervisor.

feeItems.label: HMRC premises fee
feeItems.note: Charged initially and annually per premises.

feeItems.label: HMRC BOOM approval
feeItems.note: Per beneficial owner, officer or manager approval for this sector.

gates.timing: Apply before launch where practical; current HMRC guidance describes limited trading while a complete paid application is pending.
gates.renewal: Annual declaration, details update and premises fee; missing the declaration or payment can cancel registration.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Civil penalties, publication, suspension/cancellation, responsible-person prohibitions and criminal prosecution can follow.
notEquivalentTo: Professional qualification, competence endorsement, HMRC agent registration or permission for reserved work.
gates.leastFriction: For universal rates/information, remain genuinely non-personalised. For a client-specific B2B calculator, broad HMRC supervision is often simpler and safer than trying to prove the narrow all-supervised-customer exception for every buyer.

evidence.locator: ASP scope, automation and B2B exception

gates.name: UK data-protection duties and controller fee
gates.trigger: Processing identifiable client, account, billing, security or product-usage data; the fee applies to non-exempt controllers.
steps: Map controller and processor roles.
Assess the fee and pay if not exempt.
Publish privacy information and establish lawful basis, contracts, security, retention, rights and breach handling.
Review subprocessors and international transfers.
requirements: Data minimisation, access controls, Article 28 terms where acting as processor, rights handling and a 72-hour reportable-breach process.

feeItems.label: ICO tier 1 fee
feeItems.note: Normally where turnover is at most £632,000 or staff at most ten; £5 direct-debit discount available.

feeItems.label: ICO tier 2 fee
feeItems.note: Normally where turnover is at most £36m or staff at most 250.

feeItems.label: ICO tier 3 fee
feeItems.note: Larger controllers.

gates.timing: Before or when non-exempt controllership begins; privacy/security design should precede production data.
gates.renewal: Fee every twelve months and continuous law compliance.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Non-payment fixed penalties and wider investigation, enforcement notices or UK GDPR monetary penalties can follow; paying the fee does not cure bad processing.
notEquivalentTo: Security certification, product approval or exemption from UK GDPR when no fee is due.
gates.leastFriction: Make calculations stateless or pseudonymous where possible, do not store HMRC passwords or raw tax documents by default, and register at tier 1 when the fee test is met rather than designing around a trivial annual fee.

evidence.locator: Fee tiers, exemptions, register and responsibilities
Privacy, security and software handling terms

gates.name: Mandatory HMRC registration for paid HMRC-facing tax advisers
gates.trigger: A legal entity is paid to interact with HMRC about another person's tax affairs, unless a statutory exclusion applies.
steps: Identify the entity and phased registration window.
Confirm AML supervision and suitability/tax-compliance conditions for relevant people.
Apply through the Agent Services Account route with business identifiers and evidence.
Keep conditions true and respond to HMRC monitoring.
requirements: AML supervision, tax compliance and no disqualifying conviction, sanction, insolvency or director-disqualification condition for relevant people.
The entity registers; ordinary employees do not each register separately.

feeItems.label: HMRC adviser registration
feeItems.note: HMRC states the registration is free.

gates.timing: Phased from 18 May 2026 through cohort windows ending by 31 March 2027; use the current checker rather than one universal start date.
gates.renewal: No fixed annual relicensing cycle is stated, but suitability and HMRC monitoring continue.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Compliance notices, £5,000 then potentially £10,000 penalties, temporary or permanent ineligibility and client-notification duties can apply under the new regime.
notEquivalentTo: Regulation of all tax advice, competence testing, public endorsement or client authority.
gates.leastFriction: Keep TaxSorted's initial API behind the adviser of record: calculate and export while the law/accountancy firm submits through its own HMRC account. A software-only exclusion may apply to HMRC interaction solely in that capacity, but does not remove AML or developer terms.

gates.name: Client authority for each HMRC service
gates.trigger: An agent wants HMRC to disclose or accept actions for a particular client and service.
steps: Use the digital handshake, service-specific online route or form 64-8 as applicable.
Client confirms authority.
Store service scope and revocation state without storing the client's login credentials.
requirements: A separate authority for the client and relevant service; an agent account by itself is insufficient.

feeItems.label: Client authority
feeItems.note: No HMRC application fee.

gates.timing: Before the HMRC interaction; digital-handshake links currently expire after 21 days if not accepted.
gates.renewal: No fixed term, but the client or agent may revoke and service changes can require new authority.
gates.refusalOrBreach: HMRC will restrict disclosure/action; misuse of credentials or false authority can cause access, civil, criminal and professional consequences.
notEquivalentTo: Transfer of the taxpayer's legal responsibility, adviser competence or firm registration.
gates.leastFriction: Let the customer law/accountancy firm remain agent of record. If TaxSorted later acts as agent, use supported handshakes/OAuth and a per-client capability record; never ask for the client's HMRC password.

evidence.locator: Authority methods, expiry, revocation and responsibility
Agent account separate from client authority

gates.name: ATT admission and continuing membership
gates.trigger: Using ATT designatory letters or presenting as an ATT member.
steps: Pass or receive valid credits for all assessments.
Complete two years' relevant experience.
Obtain a referee and two sponsors meeting current rules.
Make fit-and-suitable declarations, pay admission and keep annual membership/CPD.
requirements: Qualification passes, experience, relationships for verification, suitability and recurring compliance.

feeItems.label: ATT admission
feeItems.note: After qualification/experience.

feeItems.label: ATT annual member subscription
feeItems.note: To retain membership.

gates.timing: Apply after assessments and experience; sponsor relationships must satisfy the current duration rule.
gates.renewal: Annual subscription, CPD, declarations and conduct; member-in-practice rules add more duties.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Admission may be refused; discipline can reprimand, fine, suspend or expel a member under body rules.
notEquivalentTo: Parliamentary permission for ordinary tax work or HMRC endorsement.
gates.leastFriction: Accumulate experience and sponsor relationships while studying in employment, then join only when the designation and professional framework add value to the intended market.

gates.name: CIOT admission and continuing CTA membership
gates.trigger: Using Chartered Tax Adviser or CTA designation.
steps: Complete an accepted CTA route.
Document three years' relevant experience and sponsors.
Pass suitability/admission review.
Pay annual subscription and comply with CPD, conduct and practice rules.
requirements: CTA qualification, experience, sponsors, suitability and recurring professional obligations.

feeItems.label: CIOT associate subscription
feeItems.note: Current CTA associate annual fee; admission and practice costs may be additional.

gates.timing: After exams and three years' experience; exams and work commonly run concurrently.
gates.renewal: Annual subscription plus CPD, conduct and member-in-practice compliance.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Admission can be refused and members can be disciplined or lose status under the body rules.
notEquivalentTo: A statutory monopoly over tax advice, HMRC registration or permission for reserved activities.
gates.leastFriction: Work in tax while taking the direct route so experience and study overlap; do not pay for chartered status if the intended role needs only open-entry information or calculation work.

gates.name: Member-body permission for public practice
gates.trigger: A member of ATT/CIOT/ICAEW/ACCA/AAT enters public practice under that body's designation or rules.
steps: Identify the member body's definition of public practice.
Apply for its certificate, licence or member-in-practice registration.
Demonstrate approved services/experience, PII, AML, continuity and practice controls.
Pay and renew; keep the public-directory wording accurate.
requirements: Body-specific experience and competence plus PII/AML/CPD and governance.
For an AAT licence, pass separate 30-minute AML, ethics and practice-management tests at 71%, with three attempts before withdrawal.

feeItems.label: AAT bookkeeper licence
feeItems.note: For the lower service tier.

feeItems.label: AAT accountant licence
feeItems.note: Reduced to £249 under the published low-fee-income condition.

gates.timing: Before or when the member begins activity caught by that body's public-practice definition.
gates.renewal: Normally annual, with CPD, PII, AML and declarations continuing.
gates.refusalOrBreach: The body may refuse, condition, suspend or discipline the member and require cessation of designation-based public practice.
notEquivalentTo: A universal state licence imposed on non-members for all unreserved accountancy/tax work.
gates.leastFriction: Choose the body and service tier that matches actual work. An AAT bookkeeping licence is cheaper but narrower; a non-member can legally do much unreserved work but loses the designation, body framework and directory signal.

evidence.locator: AAT member licence, services and controls
Three tests, 71 percent and three-attempt rule

gates.name: Professional indemnity and technology errors-and-omissions cover
gates.trigger: Required by a selected professional/regulatory route or contract; prudent for independent tax calculation and sensitive-data risk even where not universally mandated.
steps: Map advice, calculation, cyber, contractual and reserved-activity exposures.
Obtain a policy whose scope, limits, excess, exclusions and run-off match the work.
Align contract caps and incident obligations with cover and renew without gaps.
requirements: Minimum limits and run-off differ by body; premium is market-underwritten rather than a fixed regulator fee.
gates.timing: Before the body certificate or regulated practice starts; before material independent client risk as a prudential choice.
gates.renewal: Normally annual, with notification and run-off duties continuing.
gates.refusalOrBreach: A body may refuse/suspend practice permission; an uncovered firm bears claims itself. Insurance is not a defence to unlawful work.
notEquivalentTo: Competence certification, legal authorisation or a guarantee that a claim will be paid.
gates.leastFriction: For TaxSorted, buy technology E&O/PII and cyber cover sized to the B2B API, then keep high-liability reserved acts outside the product. Do not join a body solely to obtain cover if the insurer can underwrite the actual open-entry risk directly.

gates.name: Solicitor qualification and admission in England and Wales
gates.trigger: Using the protected solicitor title or personally exercising reserved legal rights that require solicitor authorisation.
steps: Obtain a degree/equivalent.
Pass SQE1 and SQE2.
Complete and confirm two years' QWE.
Pass character and suitability review and apply for admission.
Maintain a practising certificate for practice.
requirements: Education/equivalence, assessments, qualifying work, confirmer and suitability.
gates.timing: At least two years once QWE is included; assessment sequencing and attempts can lengthen it.
gates.renewal: Annual practising certificate and continuing competence after admission.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Admission can be refused on suitability grounds; unauthorised use or reserved work can create criminal, contempt, civil and regulatory consequences.
notEquivalentTo: Tax specialism, firm authorisation or permission in other UK legal jurisdictions.
gates.leastFriction: Use flexible QWE across up to four organisations and employer-funded/self-study preparation. If the desired work is only unreserved tax advice, do not incur this gate merely for market appearance.

evidence.locator: Six reserved activities and open general advice

gates.name: SRA firm authorisation
gates.trigger: Operating an England and Wales legal-services business whose services/structure require SRA authorisation.
steps: Choose recognised-body, sole-practice or licensed-body route.
Nominate eligible managers, COLP and COFA and disclose owners/controllers.
Document governance, AML, accounts, client-money and PII systems.
Apply, obtain approvals and enter the public register.
requirements: Suitable owners/managers and compliance officers, regulated governance, PII and service-specific systems.
gates.timing: Before providing services that require the entity to be authorised.
gates.renewal: Annual fee/return and continuous regulatory compliance.
gates.refusalOrBreach: SRA may refuse, condition, intervene, suspend or revoke; unauthorised reserved work has separate legal consequences.
notEquivalentTo: A requirement for every standalone tax calculation or information API.
gates.leastFriction: Let an authorised customer remain the legal provider. TaxSorted supplies data, calculation and workflow; the firm retains legal judgment, approves instruments, conducts litigation and makes reserved submissions.

gates.name: Individual insolvency-practitioner authorisation
gates.trigger: Accepting a statutory appointment as liquidator, administrator, trustee, receiver, nominee, supervisor or another reserved insolvency office.
steps: Work under an authorised practice and build qualifying experience.
Pass JIEB examinations.
Apply to an RPB with fitness, competence, PII and bond evidence.
Renew, complete CPD and accept monitoring.
requirements: JIEB, authoriser-specific experience, fit-and-proper status, statutory bond, PII, competence and annual compliance.
gates.timing: Several years; exact experience/fee requirements vary by RPB.
gates.renewal: Usually annual authorisation with ongoing CPD and periodic monitoring.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Unauthorised acting is criminal; authorisers can condition, suspend or remove authority and publish discipline.
notEquivalentTo: Passing JIEB alone, firm employment or general restructuring software support.
gates.leastFriction: Partner with an authorised IP and keep TaxSorted to calculations, data rooms and workflow. To personally take appointments, join a practice first so qualifying cases, supervision and infrastructure develop before application.

evidence.locator: Current GB RPB oversight and bond framework

gates.name: County Court certificate for taking control of goods
gates.trigger: A private individual takes control of goods in England and Wales and is not within a statutory exemption.
steps: Obtain the Level 2 Taking Control of Goods qualification or qualifying HCEO authority.
Gather references, judgment search, photographs, declarations and recent criminal-record certificate.
Arrange prescribed £10,000 bond security.
File EAC1, give notice and attend the court hearing.
requirements: Judge-assessed fitness, knowledge of law/procedure, qualification/authority, references, record evidence and security.

feeItems.label: Court certificate application
feeItems.note: Qualification, record check and bond premiums are additional; the £10,000 bond is security, not a £10,000 court fee.

gates.timing: Before taking-control work; notice and hearing time apply.
gates.renewal: Certificate lasts two years; security and fitness must continue.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Court can refuse, suspend or cancel, order training, award compensation or forfeit security. Acting without status can invalidate action and create liability.
notEquivalentTo: A writ, warrant, debt judgment or HMRC officer power.
gates.leastFriction: Do not build taking-control powers into TaxSorted. Refer or integrate with a certificated firm and retain only non-coercive notices, calculations and audit trails.

evidence.locator: Certification, knowledge, two-year term and bond

gates.name: HMRC Developer Hub production access
gates.trigger: A product calls production HMRC APIs rather than sandbox endpoints.
steps: Register the organisation and application and build against sandbox.
Implement supported OAuth and never collect user passwords.
Provide terms/privacy/accessibility and evidence of security controls.
Complete fraud-prevention-header, penetration or service-specific testing where required.
Apply for production credentials and operate within terms.
requirements: Verified organisation/responsible person, secure software, privacy/accessibility, incident handling and API-specific conformance.

feeItems.label: General HMRC developer registration
feeItems.note: No general application fee is published; build, security testing and assurance costs remain private.

gates.timing: After sandbox readiness; current terms say a production request normally must be completed within six months.
gates.renewal: Continuous compliance; credentials and access can be changed or revoked.
gates.refusalOrBreach: HMRC may refuse, suspend or revoke access; credential sharing, scraping and insecure handling can breach terms and wider law.
notEquivalentTo: Client consent, adviser registration, product correctness or HMRC endorsement.
gates.leastFriction: Launch calculation/export endpoints without HMRC connectivity. Add production APIs only when a customer need justifies the security and operating burden, using OAuth under the customer's control.

evidence.locator: Application, security, OAuth and revocation terms

gates.name: Tax-specific HMRC software recognition
gates.trigger: Marketing or operating a filing product in a tax service whose HMRC process requires recognition/listing.
steps: Identify the tax-specific technical specification and recognition instructions.
Obtain vendor identity and submit required test evidence/checklist.
Pass HMRC functional review and use the permitted recognition wording.
Renew or retest on the service timetable.
requirements: Tax-specific calculations, schema, test cases, security/operational support and version maintenance; requirements differ between SDLT, Corporation Tax, Self Assessment and MTD.
gates.timing: After the product implements the current service specification; before claiming recognised status.
gates.renewal: Service-specific; some recognition is annual and specification changes require retest.
gates.refusalOrBreach: Product may be omitted or removed from the relevant list and API access may be affected; false endorsement claims expose the supplier to contract and consumer-law risk.
notEquivalentTo: Approved, accredited, legally correct in every case or a professional tax-adviser qualification.
gates.leastFriction: Generate validated calculations and export data while customers file through their existing recognised product. Pursue recognition one tax at a time only when direct filing is commercially necessary.

evidence.locator: SDLT recognition as a tax-specific example
Developer access remains a separate gate

pathways.name: Lowest-personal-cost entry: paid assistant or apprenticeship
pathways.outcome: Paid, supervised tax work that can fund study and count toward a professional experience requirement.
pathways.startingPoint: No tax qualification; school, degree, career-change or existing office experience depending on the vacancy.
legalMinimum: Meet employment/right-to-work rules and act within supervision; no ATT/CTA/ACA/ACCA is legally required merely to start routine tax work.
marketCredibility: A live employer, real work samples and progress toward ATT/AAT/ACA/ACCA often signal more than an unsupported self-description.

steps.action: Compare apprenticeships, technician roles and graduate intakes by work quality, funded qualification, study time and repayment terms.
steps.why: The employer is the main access gate and can impose stricter grades than the qualification body.
steps.personalCost: Usually application time; no body fee until registered.

steps.action: Choose the shortest qualification that matches the intended work rather than the highest-status label.
steps.why: ATT is a direct practical tax route; AAT, ACA and ACCA fit different accountancy goals.
steps.personalCost: Often employer-funded; confirm clawback terms.
steps.time: About eighteen months to three years depending on route.

steps.action: Keep a contemporaneous experience record and identify required supervisors, referees or sponsors early.
steps.why: Experience and sign-off are often the later bottleneck.
steps.personalCost: No official fee; relationship and record-keeping burden.

steps.action: Reassess after one year whether the role actually provides qualifying work and progression.
steps.why: Time in a tax-labelled job does not automatically satisfy every body's competence rules.
steps.personalCost: None.

pathways.estimatedTime: Immediate paid entry after recruitment; roughly two to four years to a common professional milestone.
pathways.directCost: Potentially near zero personal qualification cost when the employer funds fees and materials; contract repayment and foregone flexibility remain real costs.
pathways.lowestFriction: Use paid work to make one activity do three jobs: income, experience and funded study. Do not buy a full private course before confirming a job or body route needs it.
tradeoffs: Employer selection and continuation replace personal tuition cost as the primary gate.
Work may be narrow and sign-off can depend on managers.
Employer academic filters are conventions, not universal law.
stopConditions: The vacancy does not provide qualifying work or an identified reviewer.
Training clawback, pay or hours make the funded route more costly than it appears.
The role asks a trainee to present unsupervised work as professionally authorised.

evidence.locator: Live paid apprenticeship, entry filters, duration and places

pathways.name: Independent general adviser without buying an unnecessary designation
pathways.outcome: A lawfully established practice for unreserved tax information, advice, calculation and—when separately gated—HMRC-facing services.
pathways.startingPoint: Real tax competence from work/study, a defined service and no assumption that a voluntary designation is legal permission.
legalMinimum: Activate AML, data, HMRC registration and client authority only where the actual service triggers them.
Remain outside reserved legal, insolvency and enforcement acts unless authorised.
marketCredibility: Published scope, named reviewer/experience, PII, transparent complaints route and evidence-backed calculations can build trust without pretending registration is endorsement.

steps.action: Write the service boundary in plain language: universal information, personalised calculation, advice, return preparation, HMRC interaction, or reserved work.
steps.why: Every later gate turns on activity rather than the word used in marketing.
steps.personalCost: Founder time and legal review for borderline activities.

steps.action: Choose sole trader or company and register the required person.
steps.why: Clients need a real contracting and tax person.
steps.personalCost: £0 formation as sole trader or £100 current online company incorporation.

steps.action: For personalised tax/accountancy services, obtain AML supervision or document every condition of the narrow B2B exception.
steps.why: Automation does not remove the AML trigger.
steps.personalCost: About £740 initial HMRC fees for one premises/one BOOM, plus controls and CDD.
steps.time: Prepare before launch; HMRC says review can take up to 45 days.

steps.action: Meet UK data duties and pay the controller fee if due.
steps.why: Client facts, accounts and logs are personal data even if the calculation code is unregulated.
steps.personalCost: Usually £52 or £78 annually at small-firm scale, plus security work.

steps.action: Register with HMRC only if paid to interact for clients, then obtain per-client service authority.
steps.why: Firm access and client authority are separate conditional gates.
steps.personalCost: HMRC registration free; operating controls are not.

steps.action: Buy proportionate PII/E&O and publish what the practice is—and is not—authorised to do.
steps.why: Errors can be expensive even where insurance is not universally statutory.
steps.personalCost: Market-priced premium.

pathways.estimatedTime: Days for information-only sole trading; weeks to months for personalised work with AML, data and insurance controls.
pathways.directCost: From £0 formation plus operating costs for a narrow information service; approximately £100 incorporation + £52 ICO + £740 HMRC AML for a common one-premises/one-BOOM personalised API/advice starting stack, before insurance and software.
pathways.lowestFriction: Keep the first offer narrow and true. Let qualified client firms submit to HMRC and perform reserved work; add each controlled capability only after customers justify its cost.
tradeoffs: No designation lowers fee/time barriers but can reduce buyer trust and body-backed complaints/discipline.
A narrow product avoids gates but cannot quietly perform the wider service.
Ltd liability separation does not remove personal wrongdoing, data duties or the need for adequate insurance.
stopConditions: The service relies on calling personalised work generic information to evade AML.
The business would handle client HMRC passwords.
It would draft/submit reserved instruments, conduct litigation, accept insolvency office or take control of goods without authorised partners.

evidence.locator: Open title and voluntary consumer protections
HMRC-facing registration trigger and software exclusion

pathways.name: Practical tax credential: ATT while employed
pathways.outcome: ATT assessments, two years' relevant experience and admission to use ATT designatory letters.
pathways.startingPoint: Age 16+ with no formal academic prerequisite under the reviewed qualification rules and access to relevant work.
legalMinimum: No ATT is legally required for ordinary tax work; membership is required to claim ATT status.
marketCredibility: Six assessments, documented experience, sponsors, CPD and discipline create a verifiable body-defined signal.

steps.action: Find a supervised tax role so experience runs with study.
steps.why: Employment is not a body entry rule, but it is the cheapest way to meet experience and fund tuition.
steps.personalCost: Application time; potentially zero tuition if funded.

steps.action: Register, plan three tax papers and three CBEs inside their pass-validity windows, and use official papers before buying tuition.
steps.why: The sequence avoids expired passes and makes paid help targeted.
steps.personalCost: £1,329 first-pass body fees before study materials.

steps.action: Keep experience evidence and line up a referee and two eligible sponsors before the final pass.
steps.why: Passes alone do not create membership.
steps.personalCost: No body fee until application; relationship dependence is real.

steps.action: Apply for ATT and, only if going into practice, add body-practice, AML, HMRC and client gates.
steps.why: Designation and practice permission are distinct from general law.
steps.personalCost: £69 admission + £257 annual subscription, with practice costs extra.

pathways.estimatedTime: About two to three years when work and exams overlap.
pathways.directCost: £1,329 first-pass body fees; £1,655 including reviewed admission and first annual member subscription, before tuition, manuals, resits and practice costs.
pathways.lowestFriction: Employment + self-study/past papers first + targeted tuition only where needed; keep the two-year evidence and sponsors moving from day one.
tradeoffs: Faster and cheaper than longer chartered routes but provides a different market signal.
Exemptions save time but charge exam-equivalent fees.
Pass expiry and sponsor rules can turn delay into extra cost.
stopConditions: No route to two years' relevant experience or sponsors.
Exam dates/platform changes make the planned pass-validity sequence impossible.
The intended work needs a statutory licence ATT cannot provide.

pathways.name: Advanced tax credential: CTA direct route while working
pathways.outcome: CTA exams plus three years' relevant experience and CIOT admission.
pathways.startingPoint: Direct route has no mandatory prior accounting/law award, but advanced study assumes a real foundation.
legalMinimum: CTA is voluntary for ordinary advice; it does not unlock reserved activities.
marketCredibility: Chartered designation, advanced assessments, experience, member rules, CPD and discipline.

steps.action: Assess the foundation honestly; choose direct route only if accounting, law and core tax knowledge are ready.
steps.why: The shorter formal route can be the higher-resit route without a foundation.
steps.personalCost: Diagnostic/self-study or staged Tax Pathway cost.

steps.action: Work in tax and sequence Awareness, CBEs, Advanced Technical and APS around current sittings.
steps.why: Work builds the three years while exam skills progress.
steps.personalCost: £1,686 first-pass body fees before tuition/legislation.

steps.action: Use official syllabus/past materials and select private tuition/legislation products with the body's disclaimers visible.
steps.why: The body does not teach the whole route and restricts the exam legislation products.
steps.personalCost: Commercial provider prices vary.

steps.action: Complete three years' evidence/sponsors, join CIOT and add practice/statutory gates for independent work.
steps.why: Exams, designation and lawful practice stack are separate.
steps.personalCost: £452 annual associate subscription plus admission/practice costs.

pathways.estimatedTime: About two to three years of exams; at least three years' relevant experience for membership.
pathways.directCost: £1,686 first-pass exam-body fees plus tuition and legislation; £452 annual associate membership after admission.
pathways.lowestFriction: Run work and exams concurrently. Use the direct route only when the foundation exists; otherwise the staged route can cost less than repeated advanced attempts.
tradeoffs: High specialist signal but no legal monopoly.
Tuition and permitted legislation create a private-provider dependency.
A 2027/28 redesign makes start-date versioning essential.
stopConditions: The intended role needs a legal, audit or insolvency licence instead of tax designation.
No path to three years' qualifying experience/sponsors.
The candidate is being sold advanced tuition without the assumed foundation.

pathways.name: Chartered accountancy: choose ACA or ACCA by experience gate
pathways.outcome: ACA or ACCA membership with broad accountancy training and a tax career option.
pathways.startingPoint: School, degree, apprenticeship, AAT/other prior learning or relevant work.
legalMinimum: No chartered qualification is universally required for ordinary accounts or tax advice; specific audit, insolvency and legal work have separate gates.
marketCredibility: Exams, ethics, multi-year experience, sign-off and member discipline; public-practice permission is separate.

steps.action: Choose ACA when a strong authorised-employer agreement is available; choose ACCA when experience flexibility and self-directed sequencing matter more.
steps.why: The main structural difference is who controls qualifying experience, not a simple prestige ranking.
steps.personalCost: Application time and employer dependence.

steps.action: For ACA, complete 14 exams, specialised learning, skills/ethics and 450 days under employer sign-off.
steps.why: This is the authorised-employer route.
steps.personalCost: Variable and often employer-funded; component fees published.

steps.action: For ACCA, complete 13 exams, ethics, 36 months and nine objectives with qualified supervision.
steps.why: This route permits multiple non-authorised employers but still needs competence sign-off.
steps.personalCost: Variable centre, subscription, exam, exemption and resit fees.

steps.action: After membership, obtain the relevant practising permission only if entering public practice under the body rules.
steps.why: Membership, member practice and statutory licences are distinct layers.
steps.personalCost: Certificate, PII, AML, CPD and annual costs vary.

pathways.directCost: Variable and not honestly compressible into one figure; ACA depends heavily on employer funding/new-route fees, while ACCA has centre-set exams and annual-duration effects.
pathways.lowestFriction: Choose the experience system you can actually complete. A funded authorised ACA employer is lower-friction than self-paying; without one, ACCA's multi-employer PER or AAT-to-professional progression can be more realistic.
tradeoffs: ACA concentrates control in an authorised employer; ACCA spreads it but retains supervisor sign-off.
Exemptions reduce time but can remain fee-bearing.
Neither route alone authorises every specialist service.
stopConditions: No authorised ACA agreement or qualified ACCA supervisor is realistically available.
The chosen employer cannot provide the required work/objectives.
Marketing treats member-directory listing as universal firm regulation.

pathways.name: Solicitor route only when protected or reserved legal work matters
pathways.outcome: Admission and practising route as a solicitor of England and Wales; tax specialism then develops through work.
pathways.startingPoint: Degree or equivalent in any subject plus access to legal-service work.
legalMinimum: Solicitor status is required to use the protected title; reserved work needs an authorised individual/entity. Ordinary tax advice alone does not justify this gate.
marketCredibility: Statutory register, SQE, QWE, suitability, practising certificate, firm regulation and complaints split.

steps.action: Confirm the intended work actually needs solicitor/reserved status rather than tax competence alone.
steps.why: This avoids a £5k+ assessment route for open-entry advice.
steps.personalCost: Scope/legal research.

steps.action: Accumulate two years' QWE in up to four organisations and secure a confirmer while preparing for SQE.
steps.why: Work access and confirmation are as important as exams.
steps.personalCost: Can be paid work; preparation price varies.

steps.action: Pass SQE1 then SQE2 within attempt/time limits and apply through suitability/admission.
steps.why: This creates protected status, not tax specialism.
steps.personalCost: £4,908 current first attempts; £5,092 from published autumn 2026 fees, before prep/resits.

steps.action: For an independent firm, obtain the applicable entity authorisation, PII and compliance officers.
steps.why: Individual admission does not authorise every business structure/service.
steps.personalCost: Application, annual, PII and compliance costs vary.

pathways.estimatedTime: At least two years through flexible QWE; five to six years through many apprenticeships.
pathways.directCost: £4,908 current first-attempt SQE fees, rising to £5,092 under published autumn 2026 fees, plus education/equivalence, preparation, screening, admission, resits and practice costs.
pathways.lowestFriction: Earn QWE during paid work and use self-study/employer funding. Stop if the service only needs unreserved tax advice; partner with an authorised law firm for reserved acts instead.
tradeoffs: Statutory status and reserved-work access but large exam/resit and work-confirmation barriers.
Sole assessment-provider dependency.
SQE tests broad solicitor competence, not advanced tax specialism.
stopConditions: No qualifying work or confirmer path.
The business case is only the appearance of legal authority for unreserved tax advice.
The proposed firm lacks authorised governance/PII for reserved services.

pathways.name: Paid public-service training: HMRC Tax Specialist Programme
pathways.outcome: HMRC Grade 7 tax-specialist work after structured paid training; no external designation.
pathways.startingPoint: Reviewed 2026 external entry: any 2:2 degree and no prior tax knowledge; a separate internal route exists.
legalMinimum: Employment appointment and internal authority are required to exercise HMRC powers.
marketCredibility: Competitive Civil Service selection, structured case learning and Grade 7 progression; experience is not an external licence.

steps.action: Apply in the annual campaign and complete online tests, assessment and checks.
steps.why: Selection is the gate; there is no personal tuition purchase requirement.
steps.personalCost: Application time.

steps.action: Complete learning, work placements and programme assessments while employed.
steps.why: Authority and competence develop inside the role.
steps.personalCost: Paid salary rather than student fee.
steps.time: Three years three months to four years.

steps.action: Progress to Grade 7 if programme standards are met; separately qualify if a later private-market designation is desired.
steps.why: The programme does not issue CTA/ACA/ACCA or another external award.
steps.personalCost: No external award included.

pathways.estimatedTime: Roughly three years three months to four years after selection.
pathways.directCost: No published candidate programme fee; 2026 salary starts at £37,682 nationally or £42,631 London.
pathways.lowestFriction: For a graduate wanting deep tax training without personal tuition debt, this is unusually direct: compete once, learn while salaried, and accept that the credential is the work record rather than portable letters.
tradeoffs: Strong paid training and real case access.
Competitive campaign and public-service constraints.
No external professional accreditation on completion.
stopConditions: The applicant wants immediate self-employment or a portable designation rather than public service.
The campaign's current location, degree or employment conditions cannot be met.

evidence.locator: 2026 campaign, salary, duration and no accreditation

pathways.name: Statutory insolvency office-holder route
pathways.outcome: Individual authorisation to accept statutory insolvency appointments through a chosen RPB.
pathways.startingPoint: Employment in an insolvency practice with access to higher-level case work.
legalMinimum: Only an authorised individual may hold the specified offices; JIEB alone is insufficient.
marketCredibility: JIEB, substantial supervised experience, fit-and-proper review, PII, statutory bond, CPD, monitoring and public register.

steps.action: Join an authorised practice and select the RPB whose current route fits the work/jurisdiction.
steps.why: Experience and authoriser rules differ; training without cases cannot complete the route.
steps.personalCost: Employer/application dependence.

steps.action: Build documented qualifying case experience and pass corporate/personal JIEB papers as required.
steps.why: The exams sit inside, not above, the experience gate.
steps.personalCost: Exam/tuition fees vary by year/provider.

steps.action: Arrange PII, statutory bond, fitness evidence and apply for individual authorisation.
steps.why: This is the legal permission to take appointments.
steps.personalCost: Body licence, levies, insurance and bond premiums vary.

steps.action: Renew annually, complete CPD and accept monitoring and discipline.
steps.why: Permission is maintained, not earned once forever.
steps.personalCost: Recurring licence/levy/PII/bond/CPD.

pathways.estimatedTime: Several years; no single national timeline across authorisers.
pathways.directCost: Variable multi-provider stack; no single national total. Body-specific licence figures must be labelled as examples, not universal tariffs.
pathways.lowestFriction: Keep TaxSorted as an IP's workflow/calculation supplier. For personal authorisation, get the job and case access first; let the employer fund JIEB and infrastructure where possible.
tradeoffs: Genuine statutory monopoly for appointments and high responsibility.
Experience, exam, insurance, bond and annual licence layers compound.
Professional bodies both support members and exercise delegated authorisation under state oversight.
stopConditions: No access to qualifying cases or supervisor.
The proposed business expects a company/software product to be the office-holder.
PII, bond and monitoring infrastructure cannot be maintained.

pathways.name: Certificated enforcement agent route in England and Wales
pathways.outcome: Two-year individual court certificate to take control of goods where an independent legal authority exists.
pathways.startingPoint: Relevant employment/contract opportunity, prescribed Level 2 knowledge and ability to arrange checks, references and bond.
legalMinimum: Certificate or statutory exemption plus a real writ, warrant or statutory power for each enforcement case.
marketCredibility: Judicial fitness/knowledge review, public notice/list, qualification, record evidence and £10,000 security.

steps.action: Obtain the prescribed Level 2 Taking Control of Goods qualification or qualifying HCEO authority.
steps.why: The court requires evidence of legal/procedural knowledge.
steps.personalCost: Provider-set qualification fee.

steps.action: Gather two references, judgment search, photos, declarations and recent criminal-record evidence; arrange the bond.
steps.why: Fitness and security are separate from the knowledge test.
steps.personalCost: Search/check/bond costs.

steps.action: Pay the fee, file EAC1, complete notice and attend the County Court hearing.
steps.why: A judge grants the individual status.
steps.personalCost: £377 court fee plus external costs.

steps.action: Work only under a valid enforcement authority and renew after two years.
steps.why: Certification does not create the debt or power.
steps.personalCost: Renewal, bond and compliance.

pathways.estimatedTime: Qualification, evidence and court timetable; certificate then lasts two years.
pathways.directCost: £377 current court fee plus Level 2 training/assessment, criminal-record evidence and bond premium; £10,000 is security, not necessarily cash paid to court.
pathways.lowestFriction: For a tax platform, partner with a certificated enforcement business. The platform can manage calculations, notices and evidence without giving staff coercive powers.
tradeoffs: Real enforcement authority but high conduct/liability burden.
Individual certificate plus case-specific authority.
Different regimes outside England and Wales.
stopConditions: No underlying writ, warrant or statutory authority.
The product or employer implies certification creates an HMRC power.
Bond, fitness or renewal cannot be maintained.

evidence.locator: Two-year certificate, fitness, knowledge and security

pathways.name: TaxSorted launch lane: B2B calculation and workflow API
pathways.outcome: A useful law/accountancy-firm API that exposes evidence and calculations while customers remain adviser and filer of record.
pathways.startingPoint: A private limited company, scoped tax domain, versioned rules/evidence, and customers who already hold their professional/client-facing permissions.
legalMinimum: Real trading entity and data compliance.
AML supervision or a fully evidenced exception for personalised calculations.
HMRC production/recognition only if the API actually connects/files.
No reserved legal, insolvency or enforcement acts inside the platform role.
marketCredibility: Reproducible calculations, source provenance, rule versions, human-readable explanations, security controls, insurance and explicit capability flags.

steps.action: Incorporate the product company and publish a machine-readable service boundary.
steps.why: B2B buyers need a stable contracting person and truthful capability model.
steps.personalCost: £100 current digital incorporation plus recurring filings.

steps.action: Minimise identifiers, sign processor terms, pay the ICO fee if due, and implement retention/deletion/security.
steps.why: Tax facts are sensitive even when the software is not the adviser.
steps.personalCost: Usually £52 small-controller fee plus engineering/legal work.

steps.action: For named-client calculations, register for HMRC AML supervision or prove every condition of the all-supervised-customer exception.
steps.why: HMRC includes automated customer-specific calculation in ASP scope.
steps.personalCost: About £740 initial HMRC fees for one premises/one BOOM if supervised by HMRC.
steps.time: Prepare pre-launch; review can take up to 45 days.

steps.action: Launch calculation, validation and export only; customer submits through its own ASA/recognised product.
steps.why: This provides immediate value without taking client authority or production API burden.
steps.personalCost: Product build and insurance.

steps.action: Add OAuth production APIs only after security, accessibility, fraud-header and operational controls pass.
steps.why: Production access is a continuous platform gate, not a trophy.
steps.personalCost: No general HMRC fee; testing/assurance cost varies.

steps.action: Seek one tax-specific recognition only when direct filing demand exceeds its maintenance cost.
steps.why: Recognition is service-specific and does not certify the whole product.
steps.personalCost: Build, test and annual maintenance; no universal tariff.

steps.action: Buy technology E&O/PII and cyber cover and align contract caps with it.
steps.why: A wrong calculation or incident can create loss without any protected-title issue.
steps.personalCost: Market premium.

pathways.estimatedTime: A narrow evidence/rates API can launch fastest; personalised B2B calculation needs AML/data controls first; direct filing is a later security/recognition programme.
pathways.directCost: Representative launch fees: £100 incorporation + £52 ICO + approximately £740 HMRC AML for one premises/one BOOM = £892, before insurance, security, hosting, legal work and product build.
pathways.lowestFriction: Make the customer the adviser and filer of record. Return versioned calculations, explanations and export payloads; never hold HMRC passwords. Add regulated capabilities as separate switches with evidence, expiry and responsible party.
tradeoffs: Narrower scope reduces regulatory and liability load but delays one-click filing.
Broad AML registration may be simpler than policing the B2B exception across every customer.
HMRC recognition improves distribution for a specific filing path but is not endorsement.
stopConditions: The product personalises results while claiming to be universal information to avoid AML.
A workflow asks for HMRC passwords or scrapes portals.
The API claims approved/accredited rather than precisely recognised for one function.
It crosses into reserved legal documents, formal insolvency control or coercive enforcement without an authorised provider.

evidence.locator: Automated personalised calculation and B2B exception

studyResources.name: ATT syllabus, guidance and sample materials
studyResources.format: Official web guidance, syllabuses and candidate material
coverage: Current assessment structure, syllabus boundaries, candidate rules and route planning.
studyResources.costAndAccess: Free to read; registration/exam and any manuals remain separate.
limitations: It is not a full taught course.
Current rules can change by sitting and must be checked against the route version.

studyResources.name: ATT past papers and answers
studyResources.format: Past question papers and published answers
coverage: Question style, topic application, timing practice and answer comparison.
limitations: Past law/rates may be obsolete.
Published answers do not predict future questions or substitute for current legislation.

studyResources.name: Listed ATT and CTA tuition providers
studyResources.format: Live classes, recorded tuition, manuals, question banks and revision products from independent providers
coverage: Structured teaching and revision across ATT/CTA syllabuses; products differ by provider and paper.
studyResources.costAndAccess: Provider-priced and often employer-funded; no one official comparable total is published.
limitations: ATT/CIOT list providers but say they do not supervise or control their courses.
Listing is not a guaranteed pass or statement of market share.

studyResources.name: CIOT CTA support, syllabus and past materials
studyResources.format: Official web guidance, syllabus, skills support and past questions
coverage: Direct-route structure, current exam skills and official candidate guidance.
studyResources.costAndAccess: Public support is free; registration, exams, tuition and legislation products are separate.
limitations: Not a complete taught course.
CTA redesign and sitting changes require version checks.

studyResources.name: Permitted online legislation for CTA examinations
studyResources.format: Exam-mode online tax legislation supplied through specified commercial student products
coverage: Permitted legislation reference in the reviewed CTA exam environment.
studyResources.costAndAccess: Separately purchased Croner-i or Tolley student product under current rules.
limitations: A private product gate, not primary law access in general.
Approved products and exam rules can change.

studyResources.name: Next Generation ACA authorised-employer handbook
studyResources.format: Official employer handbook and training-record specification
coverage: Training agreement, 14 exams, 450 days, specialised learning, skills, ethics, reviews and sign-off.
studyResources.costAndAccess: Free public handbook; learning/exams and employer delivery have separate costs.
limitations: Designed for the Next Generation route, not every legacy ACA student.
A handbook cannot supply an authorised employer or qualifying work.

studyResources.name: ACCA Study Hub
studyResources.format: Digital chapters, flashcards, practice questions and quizzes
coverage: Official syllabus-aligned support for eligible ACCA students.
studyResources.costAndAccess: Included/free through eligible student access; registration and exams remain charged.
limitations: Does not remove experience/supervisor or assessment fees.
Access and paper coverage depend on current eligibility.

studyResources.name: AAT approved-provider Level 4 study
studyResources.format: Classroom, online or distance learning with approved providers and computer-based assessments
coverage: Current mandatory/elective Level 4 units and the September 2026 replacement route.
studyResources.costAndAccess: AAT publishes an indicative £1,000–£3,000 Level 4 tuition range plus body fees; provider prices vary.
limitations: Approved status is route-specific and does not make one provider cheapest or best.
Current and replacement qualifications must not be mixed.

studyResources.name: AAT licensed-member standards-test materials
studyResources.format: Official e-learning and three online standards tests
coverage: AML, ethics and practice management; each test is 30 minutes with a 71% pass mark and three-attempt limit.
studyResources.costAndAccess: Access is part of the current licence application process; licence/admission fees are separate.
limitations: Tests support an AAT member licence, not the Level 4 diploma assessment itself.
Passing does not prove technical competence in every service category.

studyResources.name: SRA SQE route and assessment specification
studyResources.format: Official route, assessment specifications, sample questions and QWE guidance
coverage: SQE1/SQE2 scope, ordering, QWE, suitability and admission.
studyResources.costAndAccess: Official information is free; assessments and optional private preparation are charged.
limitations: The SRA does not prescribe a preparation course.
Provider course-finder information is supplier-provided and not a quality guarantee.

studyResources.name: Insolvency authoriser JIEB and licence guidance
studyResources.format: Authoriser route guidance plus employer/commercial JIEB tuition
coverage: JIEB within the broader experience, PII, bond, fitness and licence route.
studyResources.costAndAccess: Authoriser guidance is public; exam and tuition prices depend on year/provider.
limitations: One RPB's route is not a universal tariff or experience rule.
Exam preparation does not create statutory appointment access.

compensation.label: Live London tax-consultant apprenticeship salary
